mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter expansion rate increasing) signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60-trading-day period through multiple earnings revision cycles.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 vintage 23703.782 (revised 23770.976) indicates ongoing economic activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 growth quarters do not produce statistically significantly higher 60-day forward returns than negative growth quarters (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when the rate remains elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of 2025-08-01, near restrictive threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers negative equity returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% as of 2025-08-01, historically moderate level
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both declining simultaneously signal weakening consumer demand and construction activity, indicating late-cycle contraction. This joint deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance revisions cascade.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) does not explain additional variance beyond single-series models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth acceleration signals strengthening supply-side capacity and manufacturing momentum, reducing inflation pressures and supporting equity valuations. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in non-inflationary regimes.
failure_condition: When INDPRO growth > 0.3% month-over-month, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate moving in opposite directions (inflation rising while rates fall, or inflation falling while rates rise) signals policy accommodation or tightening misalignment. This creates equity volatility and negative returns over 20 trading days as real rate expectations shift.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS diverge directionally over 2 months
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS diverge directionally, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect does not explain additional variance beyond single-series models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100K new jobs per month signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer income growth. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings and consumption forecasts decline.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100K for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 100K for 2 consecutive months, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) both accelerating simultaneously signal broad-based demand expansion across residential and aggregate sectors. This joint acceleration produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through sustained earnings growth and multiple expansion.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and GDPC1 both show quarter-over-quarter acceleration
failure_condition: When HOUST and GDPC1 both accelerate, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in < 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect does not explain additional variance beyond single-series models (R² improvement < 0.02).
